Scheduler always tries to find\n147: \t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n148: \t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n149: \t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n150: \t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n151: \t// nodes will be scored. In kube-scheduler, after\\n39: \\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\\n40: \\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\\n41: \\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\\n42: \\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\\n43: \\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\\n44: \\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\\n45: \\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\\n46: \\tmetav1.TypeMeta\\n47: \\n48: \\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\\n49: \\tParallelism int32\\n50: \\n51: \\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\\n52: \\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\\n53: \\n54: \\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\\n55: \\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\\n56: \\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\\n57: \\n58: \\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\\n59: \\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n60: \\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n61: \\n62: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n63: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n64: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n65: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n66: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n67: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n68: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n69: \\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\\n70: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n71: \\n72: \\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n73: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n74: \\t// will be used.\\n75: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\\n76: \\n77: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n78: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n79: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n80: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\\n81: \\n82: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\\n83: \\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\\n84: \\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n85: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\tExtenders []Extender\\n91: \\n92: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n93: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n94: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n95: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n96: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n101: \\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\\n102: \\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \\\"spec.schedulerName\\\", then the pod\\n103: \\t// is scheduled with this profile.\\n104: \\tSchedulerName string\\n105: \\n106: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n107: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n108: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n109: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n110: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n111: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n112: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n113: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n114: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n115: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n118: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n119: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n120: \\t// should be disabled.\\n121: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n122: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n123: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n124: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n125: \\tPlugins *Plugins\\n126: \\n127: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n128: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n129: \\t// for that plugin.\\n130: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n138: type Plugins struct {\\n139: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n140: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet\\n141: \\n142: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n143: \\tQueueSort PluginSet\\n144: \\n145: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n146: \\tPreFilter PluginSet\\n147: \\n148: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n149: \\tFilter PluginSet\\n150: \\n151: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n152: \\tPostFilter PluginSet\\n153: \\n154: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n155: \\tPreScore PluginSet\\n156: \\n157: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n158: \\tScore PluginSet\\n159: \\n160: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n161: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n162: \\tReserve PluginSet\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n165: \\tPermit PluginSet\\n166: \\n167: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n168: \\tPreBind PluginSet\\n169: \\n170: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n171: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n172: \\tBind PluginSet\\n173: \\n174: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n175: \\tPostBind PluginSet\\n176: \\n177: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\\n178: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n183: type PluginSet struct {\\n184: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n185: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n186: \\tEnabled []Plugin\\n187: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n188: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n189: \\tDisabled []Plugin\\n190: }\\n191: \\n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n193: type Plugin struct {\\n194: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n195: \\tName string\\n196: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n197: \\tWeight int32\\n198: }\\n199: \\n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\\n204: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n205: \\tName string\\n206: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n207: \\tArgs runtime.Object\\n208: }\\n209: \\n210: /*\\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\\n212:  */\\n213: const (\\n214: \\t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\\n215: \\t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\\n216: \\t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\\n217: \\tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\\n218: \\n219: \\t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\\n220: \\tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\\n221: \\n222: \\t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\\n223: \\tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\\n224: \\n225: \\t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\\n226: \\tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\\n227: )\\n228: \\n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\\n231: \\tif p == nil {\\n232: \\t\\treturn nil\\n233: \\t}\\n234: \\textensions := []PluginSet{\\n235: \\t\\tp.PreEnqueue,\\n236: \\t\\tp.PreFilter,\\n237: \\t\\tp.Filter,\\n238: \\t\\tp.PostFilter,\\n239: \\t\\tp.Reserve,\\n240: \\t\\tp.PreScore,\\n241: \\t\\tp.Score,\\n242: \\t\\tp.PreBind,\\n243: \\t\\tp.Bind,\\n244: \\t\\tp.PostBind,\\n245: \\t\\tp.Permit,\\n246: \\t\\tp.QueueSort,\\n247: \\t}\\n248: \\tn := sets.New[string]()\\n249: \\tfor _, e := range extensions {\\n250: \\t\\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\\n251: \\t\\t\\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\\n252: \\t\\t}\\n253: \\t}\\n254: \\treturn sets.List(n)\\n255: }\\n256: \\n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n259: type Extender struct {\\n260: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n261: \\tURLPrefix string\\n262: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n263: \\tFilterVerb string\\n264: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n265: \\tPreemptVerb string\\n266: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n267: \\tPrioritizeVerb string\\n268: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n269: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n270: \\tWeight int64\\n271: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n272: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n273: \\t// can implement this function.\\n274: \\tBindVerb string\\n275: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n276: \\tEnableHTTPS bool\\n277: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n278: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\\n279: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\\n280: \\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\\n281: \\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\\n282: \\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\\n283: \\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\\n284: \\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\\n285: \\tNodeCacheCapable bool\\n286: \\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\\n287: \\t// this extender.\\n288: \\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\\n289: \\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\\n290: \\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\\n291: \\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\\n292: \\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\\n293: \\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\\n294: \\t// +optional\\n295: \\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\\n296: \\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\\n297: \\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\\n298: \\tIgnorable bool\\n299: }\\n300: \\n301: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\\n302: // managed by an extender.\\n303: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\\n304: \\t// Name is the extended resource name.\\n305: \\tName string\\n306: \\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\\n307: \\t// resource when applying predicates.\\n308: \\tIgnoredByScheduler bool\\n309: }\\n310: \\n311: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\\n312: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\\n313: \\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\\n314: \\tInsecure bool\\n315: \\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\\n316: \\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\\n317: \\t// server is used.\\n318: \\tServerName string\\n319: \\n320: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n321: \\tCertFile string\\n322: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n323: \\tKeyFile string\\n324: \\t// Trusted root certificates for server\\n325: \\tCAFile string\\n326: \\n327: \\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\\n328: \\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\\n329: \\tCertData []byte\\n330: \\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\\n331: \\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\\n332: \\tKeyData []byte `datapolicy:\\\"security-key\\\"`\\n333: \\t// CAData \\\"k8s.io/component-base/config\\\"\\n26: )\\n27: \\n28: const (\\n29: \\t// DefaultKubeSchedulerPort is the default port for the scheduler status server.\\n30: \\t// May be overridden by a flag at startup.\\n31: \\tDefaultKubeSchedulerPort = 10259\\n32: )\\n33: \\n34: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\\n35: \\n36: // KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\\n37: type KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\\n38: \\t// TypeMeta contains the API version and kind. In kube-scheduler, after\\n39: \\t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\\n40: \\t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\\n41: \\t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\\n42: \\t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\\n43: \\t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\\n44: \\t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\\n45: \\t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\\n46: \\tmetav1.TypeMeta\\n47: \\n48: \\t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\\n49: \\tParallelism int32\\n50: \\n51: \\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\\n52: \\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\\n53: \\n54: \\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\\n55: \\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\\n56: \\tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\\n57: \\n58: \\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\\n59: \\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n60: \\tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\\n61: \\n62: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n63: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n64: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n65: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n66: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n67: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n68: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n69: \\t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\\n70: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n71: \\n72: \\t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n73: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n74: \\t// will be used.\\n75: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\\n76: \\n77: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n78: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n79: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n80: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\\n81: \\n82: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\\n83: \\t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\\n84: \\t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n85: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\tExtenders []Extender\\n91: \\n92: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n93: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n94: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n95: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n96: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n101: \\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\\n102: \\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \\\"spec.schedulerName\\\", then the pod\\n103: \\t// is scheduled with this profile.\\n104: \\tSchedulerName string\\n105: \\n106: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n107: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n108: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n109: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n110: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n111: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n112: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n113: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n114: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n115: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n118: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n119: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n120: \\t// should be disabled.\\n121: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n122: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n123: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n124: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n125: \\tPlugins *Plugins\\n126: \\n127: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n128: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n129: \\t// for that plugin.\\n130: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\\n131: }\\n132: \\n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. If an extension point is\\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. \\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\\n328: \\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\\n329: \\tCertData []byte\\n330: \\t// KeyData holds \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n24: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n25: \tcomponentbaseconfig \"k8s.io/component-base/config\"\n26: )\n27: \n28: const (\n29: \t// DefaultKubeSchedulerPort is the default port for the scheduler status server.\n30: \t// May be overridden by a flag at startup.\n31: \tDefaultKubeSchedulerPort = 10259\n32: )\n33: \n34: // +k8s:deepcopy-gen:interfaces=k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime.Object\n35: \n36: // KubeSchedulerConfiguration configures a scheduler\n37: type KubeSchedulerConfiguration struct {\n38: \t// TypeMeta contains the API version and kind. In kube-scheduler, after\n39: \t// conversion from the versioned KubeSchedulerConfiguration type to this\n40: \t// internal type, we set the APIVersion field to the scheme group/version of\n41: \t// the type we converted from. This is done in cmd/kube-scheduler in two\n42: \t// places: (1) when loading config from a file, (2) generating the default\n43: \t// config. Based on the versioned type set in this field, we make decisions;\n44: \t// for example (1) during validation to check for usage of removed plugins,\n45: \t// (2) writing config to a file, (3) initialising the scheduler.\n46: \tmetav1.TypeMeta\n47: \n48: \t// Parallelism defines the amount of parallelism in algorithms for scheduling a Pods. Must be greater than 0. Defaults to 16\n49: \tParallelism int32\n50: \n51: \t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\n52: \tLeaderElection componentbaseconfig.LeaderElectionConfiguration\n53: \n54: \t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\n55: \t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\n56: \tClientConnection componentbaseconfig.ClientConnectionConfiguration\n57: \n58: \t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\n59: \t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n60: \tcomponentbaseconfig.DebuggingConfiguration\n61: \n62: \t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n63: \t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n64: \t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n65: \t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n66: \t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n67: \t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n68: \t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n69: \t// nodes will be scored. It is overridden by profile level PercentageOfNodesToScore.\n70: \tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n71: \n72: \t// PodInitialBackoffSeconds is the initial backoff for unschedulable pods.\n73: \t// If specified, it must be greater than 0. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\n74: \t// will be used.\n75: \tPodInitialBackoffSeconds int64\n76: \n77: \t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\n78: \t// If specified, it must be greater than or equal to podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\n79: \t// the default value (10s) will be used.\n80: \tPodMaxBackoffSeconds int64\n81: \n82: \t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods can\n83: \t// choose to be scheduled under a particular profile by setting its associated\n84: \t// scheduler name. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\n85: \t// with the \"default-scheduler\" profile, if present here.\n86: \tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile\n87: \n88: \t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\n89: \t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\n90: \tExtenders []Extender\n91: \n92: \t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n93: \t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n94: \t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n95: \t// Defaults to false.\n96: \tDelayCacheUntilActive bool\n97: }\n98: \n99: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\n100: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\n101: \t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n102: \t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n103: \t// is scheduled with this profile.\n104: \tSchedulerName string\n105: \n106: \t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n107: \t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n108: \t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n109: \t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n110: \t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n111: \t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n112: \t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n113: \t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n114: \t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n115: \tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32\n116: \n117: \t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n118: \t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n119: \t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n120: \t// should be disabled.\n121: \t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n122: \t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n123: \t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n124: \t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n125: \tPlugins *Plugins\n126: \n127: \t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n128: \t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n129: \t// for that plugin.\n130: \tPluginConfig []PluginConfig\n131: }\n132: \n133: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n134: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n135: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n136: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n137: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n138: type Plugins struct {\n139: \t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n140: \tPreEnqueue PluginSet\n141: \n142: \t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n143: \tQueueSort PluginSet\n144: \n145: \t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n146: \tPreFilter PluginSet\n147: \n148: \t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n149: \tFilter PluginSet\n150: \n151: \t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n152: \tPostFilter PluginSet\n153: \n154: \t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n155: \tPreScore PluginSet\n156: \n157: \t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n158: \tScore PluginSet\n159: \n160: \t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n161: \t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n162: \tReserve PluginSet\n163: \n164: \t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n165: \tPermit PluginSet\n166: \n167: \t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n168: \tPreBind PluginSet\n169: \n170: \t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n171: \t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n172: \tBind PluginSet\n173: \n174: \t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n175: \tPostBind PluginSet\n176: \n177: \t// MultiPoint is a simplified config field for enabling plugins for all valid extension points\n178: \tMultiPoint PluginSet\n179: }\n180: \n181: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n182: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n183: type PluginSet struct {\n184: \t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n185: \t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n186: \tEnabled []Plugin\n187: \t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n188: \t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n189: \tDisabled []Plugin\n190: }\n191: \n192: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\n193: type Plugin struct {\n194: \t// Name defines the name of plugin\n195: \tName string\n196: \t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n197: \tWeight int32\n198: }\n199: \n200: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n201: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n202: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\n203: type PluginConfig struct {\n204: \t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n205: \tName string\n206: \t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n207: \tArgs runtime.Object\n208: }\n209: \n210: /*\n211:  * NOTE: The following variables and methods are intentionally left out of the staging mirror.\n212:  */\n213: const (\n214: \t// DefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore defines the percentage of nodes of all nodes\n215: \t// that once found feasible, the scheduler stops looking for more nodes.\n216: \t// A value of 0 means adaptive, meaning the scheduler figures out a proper default.\n217: \tDefaultPercentageOfNodesToScore = 0\n218: \n219: \t// MaxCustomPriorityScore is the max score UtilizationShapePoint expects.\n220: \tMaxCustomPriorityScore int64 = 10\n221: \n222: \t// MaxTotalScore is the maximum total score.\n223: \tMaxTotalScore int64 = math.MaxInt64\n224: \n225: \t// MaxWeight defines the max weight value allowed for custom PriorityPolicy\n226: \tMaxWeight = MaxTotalScore / MaxCustomPriorityScore\n227: )\n228: \n229: // Names returns the list of enabled plugin names.\n230: func (p *Plugins) Names() []string {\n231: \tif p == nil {\n232: \t\treturn nil\n233: \t}\n234: \textensions := []PluginSet{\n235: \t\tp.PreEnqueue,\n236: \t\tp.PreFilter,\n237: \t\tp.Filter,\n238: \t\tp.PostFilter,\n239: \t\tp.Reserve,\n240: \t\tp.PreScore,\n241: \t\tp.Score,\n242: \t\tp.PreBind,\n243: \t\tp.Bind,\n244: \t\tp.PostBind,\n245: \t\tp.Permit,\n246: \t\tp.QueueSort,\n247: \t}\n248: \tn := sets.New[string]()\n249: \tfor _, e := range extensions {\n250: \t\tfor _, pg := range e.Enabled {\n251: \t\t\tn.Insert(pg.Name)\n252: \t\t}\n253: \t}\n254: \treturn sets.List(n)\n255: }\n256: \n257: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n258: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\n259: type Extender struct {\n260: \t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n261: \tURLPrefix string\n262: \t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n263: \tFilterVerb string\n264: \t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n265: \tPreemptVerb string\n266: \t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n267: \tPrioritizeVerb string\n268: \t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n269: \t// The weight should be a positive integer\n270: \tWeight int64\n271: \t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n272: \t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n273: \t// can implement this function.\n274: \tBindVerb string\n275: \t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n276: \tEnableHTTPS bool\n277: \t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n278: \tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig\n279: \t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n280: \t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n281: \tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration\n282: \t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n283: \t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n284: \t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n285: \tNodeCacheCapable bool\n286: \t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n287: \t// this extender.\n288: \t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n289: \t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n290: \t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n291: \t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n292: \t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n293: \t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n294: \t// +optional\n295: \tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource\n296: \t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n297: \t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n298: \tIgnorable bool\n299: }\n300: \n301: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n302: // managed by an extender.\n303: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n304: \t// Name is the extended resource name.\n305: \tName string\n306: \t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n307: \t// resource when applying predicates.\n308: \tIgnoredByScheduler bool\n309: }\n310: \n311: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\n312: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n313: \t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n314: \tInsecure bool\n315: \t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n316: \t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\n317: \t// server is used.\n318: \tServerName string\n319: \n320: \t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n321: \tCertFile string\n322: \t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n323: \tKeyFile string\n324: \t// Trusted root certificates for server\n325: \tCAFile string\n326: \n327: \t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\n328: \t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\n329: \tCertData []byte\n330: \t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\n331: \t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\n332: \tKeyData []byte `datapolicy:\"security-key\"`\n333: \t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\n334: \t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\n335: \tCAData []byte\n336: }\n337: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0@917ed05e89979783903c4ca4563f0fc4120df4e2/-/blob/pkg/scheduler/apis/config/types.go"}}}

\\t\\t\\t}\\n132: \\t\\t}\\n133: \\t}\\n134: \\treturn nil\\n135: }\\n136: \\n137: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n138: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n139: \\t// SchedulerName running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n146: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n147: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n148: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n149: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n150: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n151: \\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\\n152: \\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\\n153: \\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:\\\"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty\\\"`\\n154: \\n155: \\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\\n156: \\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\\n157: \\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\\n158: \\t// should be disabled.\\n159: \\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\\n160: \\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\\n161: \\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\\n162: \\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\\n163: \\tPlugins *Plugins `json:\\\"plugins,omitempty\\\"`\\n164: \\n165: \\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\\n166: \\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\\n167: \\t// for that plugin.\\n168: \\t// +listType=map\\n169: \\t// +listMapKey=name\\n170: \\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig `json:\\\"pluginConfig,omitempty\\\"`\\n171: }\\n172: \\n173: // Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\\n174: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n175: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n176: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n177: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n178: type Plugins struct {\\n179: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n180: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet `json:\\\"preEnqueue,omitempty\\\"`\\n181: \\n182: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n183: \\tQueueSort PluginSet `json:\\\"queueSort,omitempty\\\"`\\n184: \\n185: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n186: \\tPreFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"preFilter,omitempty\\\"`\\n187: \\n188: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n189: \\tFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"filter,omitempty\\\"`\\n190: \\n191: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n192: \\tPostFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"postFilter,omitempty\\\"`\\n193: \\n194: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n195: \\tPreScore PluginSet `json:\\\"preScore,omitempty\\\"`\\n196: \\n197: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n198: \\tScore PluginSet `json:\\\"score,omitempty\\\"`\\n199: \\n200: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n201: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n202: \\tReserve PluginSet `json:\\\"reserve,omitempty\\\"`\\n203: \\n204: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n205: \\tPermit PluginSet `json:\\\"permit,omitempty\\\"`\\n206: \\n207: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n208: \\tPreBind PluginSet `json:\\\"preBind,omitempty\\\"`\\n209: \\n210: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n211: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n212: \\tBind PluginSet `json:\\\"bind,omitempty\\\"`\\n213: \\n214: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n215: \\tPostBind PluginSet `json:\\\"postBind,omitempty\\\"`\\n216: \\n217: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config section to enable plugins for all valid extension points.\\n218: \\t// Plugins enabled through MultiPoint will automatically register for every individual extension\\n219: \\t// point the plugin has implemented. Disabling a plugin through MultiPoint disables that behavior.\\n220: \\t// The same is true for disabling \\\"*\\\" through MultiPoint (no default plugins will be automatically registered).\\n221: \\t// Plugins can still be disabled through their individual extension points.\\n222: \\t//\\n223: \\t// In terms of precedence, plugin config follows this basic hierarchy\\n224: \\t//   1. Specific extension points\\n225: \\t//   2. Explicitly configured MultiPoint plugins\\n226: \\t//   3. The set of default plugins, as MultiPoint plugins\\n227: \\t// This implies that a higher precedence plugin will run first and overwrite any settings within MultiPoint.\\n228: \\t// Explicitly user-configured plugins also take a higher precedence over default plugins.\\n229: \\t// Within this hierarchy, an Enabled setting takes precedence over Disabled. For example, if a plugin is\\n230: \\t// set in both `multiPoint.Enabled` and `multiPoint.Disabled`, the plugin will be enabled. Similarly,\\n231: \\t// including `multiPoint.Disabled = '*'` and `multiPoint.Enabled = pluginA` will still register that specific\\n232: \\t// plugin through MultiPoint. This follows the same behavior as all other extension point configurations.\\n233: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet `json:\\\"multiPoint,omitempty\\\"`\\n234: }\\n235: \\n236: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n237: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n238: type PluginSet struct {\\n239: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n240: \\t// If the default plugin is also configured in the scheduler config file, the weight of plugin will\\n241: \\t// be overridden accordingly.\\n242: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n243: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n244: \\tEnabled []Plugin `json:\\\"enabled,omitempty\\\"`\\n245: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n246: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n247: \\t// +listType=map\\n248: \\t// +listMapKey=name\\n249: \\tDisabled []Plugin `json:\\\"disabled,omitempty\\\"`\\n250: }\\n251: \\n252: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n253: type Plugin struct {\\n254: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n255: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n256: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n257: \\tWeight *int32 `json:\\\"weight,omitempty\\\"`\\n258: }\\n259: \\n260: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n261: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n262: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n263: type PluginConfig struct {\\n264: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n265: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n266: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n267: \\tArgs runtime.RawExtension `json:\\\"args,omitempty\\\"`\\n268: }\\n269: \\n270: func (c *PluginConfig) decodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\\n271: \\tgvk := SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(c.Name + \\\"Args\\\")\\n272: \\t// dry-run to detect and skip out-of-tree plugin args.\\n273: \\tif _, _, err := d.Decode(nil, &gvk, nil); runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err) {\\n274: \\t\\treturn nil\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\tvar strictDecodingErr error\\n278: \\tobj, parsedGvk, err := d.Decode(c.Args.Raw, &gvk, nil)\\n279: \\tif err != nil {\\n280: \\t\\tdecodingArgsErr := fmt.Errorf(\\\"decoding args for plugin %s: %w\\\", c.Name, err)\\n281: \\t\\tif obj != nil && runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\\n282: \\t\\t\\tstrictDecodingErr = runtime.NewStrictDecodingError([]error{decodingArgsErr})\\n283: \\t\\t} else {\\n284: \\t\\t\\treturn decodingArgsErr\\n285: \\t\\t}\\n286: \\t}\\n287: \\tif parsedGvk.GroupKind() != gvk.GroupKind() {\\n288: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"args for plugin %s were not of type %s, got %s\\\", c.Name, gvk.GroupKind(), parsedGvk.GroupKind())\\n289: \\t}\\n290: \\tc.Args.Object = obj\\n291: \\treturn strictDecodingErr\\n292: }\\n293: \\n294: func (c *PluginConfig) encodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\\n295: \\tif c.Args.Object == nil {\\n296: \\t\\treturn nil\\n297: \\t}\\n298: \\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\\n299: \\terr := e.Encode(c.Args.Object, &buf)\\n300: \\tif err != nil {\\n301: \\t\\treturn err\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\t// The <e> encoder might be a YAML encoder, but the parent encoder expects\\n304: \\t// JSON output, so we convert YAML back to JSON.\\n305: \\t// This is a no-op if <e> produces JSON.\\n306: \\tjson, err := yaml.YAMLToJSON(buf.Bytes())\\n307: \\tif err != nil {\\n308: \\t\\treturn err\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\tc.Args.Raw = json\\n311: \\treturn nil\\n312: }\\n313: \\n314: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n315: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n316: type Extender struct {\\n317: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n318: \\tURLPrefix string `json:\\\"urlPrefix\\\"`\\n319: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n320: \\tFilterVerb string `json:\\\"filterVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n321: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n322: \\tPreemptVerb string `json:\\\"preemptVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n323: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n324: \\tPrioritizeVerb string `json:\\\"prioritizeVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n325: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n326: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n327: \\tWeight int64 `json:\\\"weight,omitempty\\\"`\\n328: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n329: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n330: \\t// can implement this function.\\n331: \\tBindVerb string `json:\\\"bindVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n332: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n333: \\tEnableHTTPS bool `json:\\\"enableHTTPS,omitempty\\\"`\\n334: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n335: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig `json:\\\"tlsConfig,omitempty\\\"`\\n336: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\\n337: \\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\\n338: \\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration `json:\\\"httpTimeout,omitempty\\\"`\\n339: \\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\\n340: \\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\\n341: \\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\\n342: \\tNodeCacheCapable bool `json:\\\"nodeCacheCapable,omitempty\\\"`\\n343: \\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\\n344: \\t// this extender.\\n345: \\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\\n346: \\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\\n347: \\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\\n348: \\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\\n349: \\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\\n350: \\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\\n351: \\t// +optional\\n352: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n353: \\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource `json:\\\"managedResources,omitempty\\\"`\\n354: \\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\\n355: \\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\\n356: \\tIgnorable bool `json:\\\"ignorable,omitempty\\\"`\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\\n360: // managed by an extender.\\n361: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\\n362: \\t// Name is the extended resource name.\\n363: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n364: \\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\\n365: \\t// resource when applying predicates.\\n366: \\tIgnoredByScheduler bool `json:\\\"ignoredByScheduler,omitempty\\\"`\\n367: }\\n368: \\n369: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\\n370: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\\n371: \\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\\n372: \\tInsecure bool `json:\\\"insecure,omitempty\\\"`\\n373: \\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\\n374: \\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\\n375: \\t// server is used.\\n376: \\tServerName string `json:\\\"serverName,omitempty\\\"`\\n377: \\n378: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n379: \\tCertFile string `json:\\\"certFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n380: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n381: \\tKeyFile string `json:\\\"keyFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n382: \\t// Trusted root certificates for server\\n383: \\tCAFile string `json:\\\"caFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n384: \\n385: \\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\\n386: \\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\\n387: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n388: \\tCertData []byte `json:\\\"certData,omitempty\\\"`\\n389: \\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\\n390: \\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\\n391: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n392: \\tKeyData []byte `json:\\\"keyData,omitempty\\\"`\\n393: \\t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\\n394: \\t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\\n395: \\t// Defaults to 16\\n47: \\tParallelism *int32 `json:\\\"parallelism,omitempty\\\"`\\n48: \\n49: \\t// LeaderElection defines the configuration of leader election client.\\n50: \\tLeaderElection componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.LeaderElectionConfiguration `json:\\\"leaderElection\\\"`\\n51: \\n52: \\t// ClientConnection specifies the kubeconfig file and client connection\\n53: \\t// settings for the proxy server to use when communicating with the apiserver.\\n54: \\tClientConnection componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.ClientConnectionConfiguration `json:\\\"clientConnection\\\"`\\n55: \\n56: \\t// DebuggingConfiguration holds configuration for Debugging related features\\n57: \\t// TODO: We might wanna make this a substruct like Debugging componentbaseconfigv1alpha1.DebuggingConfiguration\\n58: \\tcomponentbaseconfigv1alpha1.DebuggingConfiguration `json:\\\",inline\\\"`\\n59: \\n60: \\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\\n61: \\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\\n62: \\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\\n63: \\t// at least \\\"minFeasibleNodesToFind\\\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\\n64: \\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\\n65: \\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\\n66: \\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\\n67: \\t// nodes will be scored. If this value is null, the default value (1s)\\n72: \\t// will be used.\\n73: \\tPodInitialBackoffSeconds *int64 `json:\\\"podInitialBackoffSeconds,omitempty\\\"`\\n74: \\n75: \\t// PodMaxBackoffSeconds is the max backoff for unschedulable pods.\\n76: \\t// If specified, it must be greater than podInitialBackoffSeconds. If this value is null,\\n77: \\t// the default value (10s) will be used.\\n78: \\tPodMaxBackoffSeconds *int64 `json:\\\"podMaxBackoffSeconds,omitempty\\\"`\\n79: \\n80: \\t// Profiles are scheduling profiles that kube-scheduler supports. Pods that don't specify any scheduler name are scheduled\\n83: \\t// with the \\\"default-scheduler\\\" profile, if present here.\\n84: \\t// +listType=map\\n85: \\t// +listMapKey=schedulerName\\n86: \\tProfiles []KubeSchedulerProfile `json:\\\"profiles,omitempty\\\"`\\n87: \\n88: \\t// Extenders are the list of scheduler extenders, each holding the values of how to communicate\\n89: \\t// with the extender. These extenders are shared by all scheduler profiles.\\n90: \\t// +listType=set\\n91: \\tExtenders []Extender `json:\\\"extenders,omitempty\\\"`\\n92: \\n93: \\t// DelayCacheUntilActive specifies when to start caching. If this is true and leader election is enabled,\\n94: \\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\\n95: \\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\\n96: \\t// Defaults to false.\\n97: \\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool `json:\\\"delayCacheUntilActive,omitempty\\\"`\\n98: }\\n99: \\n100: // DecodeNestedObjects decodes plugin args for known types.\\n101: func (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) DecodeNestedObjects(d \\t\\t\\t}\\n132: \\t\\t}\\n133: \\t}\\n134: \\treturn nil\\n135: }\\n136: \\n137: // KubeSchedulerProfile is a scheduling profile.\\n138: type KubeSchedulerProfile struct {\\n139: \\t// SchedulerName When specified, the list of plugins for\\n174: // a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\\n175: // omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\\n176: // Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\\n177: // be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\\n178: type Plugins struct {\\n179: \\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\\n180: \\tPreEnqueue PluginSet `json:\\\"preEnqueue,omitempty\\\"`\\n181: \\n182: \\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\\n183: \\tQueueSort PluginSet `json:\\\"queueSort,omitempty\\\"`\\n184: \\n185: \\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"PreFilter\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n186: \\tPreFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"preFilter,omitempty\\\"`\\n187: \\n188: \\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\\n189: \\tFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"filter,omitempty\\\"`\\n190: \\n191: \\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\\n192: \\tPostFilter PluginSet `json:\\\"postFilter,omitempty\\\"`\\n193: \\n194: \\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\\n195: \\tPreScore PluginSet `json:\\\"preScore,omitempty\\\"`\\n196: \\n197: \\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\\n198: \\tScore PluginSet `json:\\\"score,omitempty\\\"`\\n199: \\n200: \\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\\n201: \\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\\n202: \\tReserve PluginSet `json:\\\"reserve,omitempty\\\"`\\n203: \\n204: \\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\\n205: \\tPermit PluginSet `json:\\\"permit,omitempty\\\"`\\n206: \\n207: \\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\\n208: \\tPreBind PluginSet `json:\\\"preBind,omitempty\\\"`\\n209: \\n210: \\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \\\"Bind\\\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\\n211: \\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\\n212: \\tBind PluginSet `json:\\\"bind,omitempty\\\"`\\n213: \\n214: \\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\\n215: \\tPostBind PluginSet `json:\\\"postBind,omitempty\\\"`\\n216: \\n217: \\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config section to enable plugins for all valid extension points.\\n218: \\t// Plugins enabled through MultiPoint will automatically register for every individual extension\\n219: \\t// point the plugin has implemented. Disabling a plugin through MultiPoint disables that behavior.\\n220: \\t// The same is true for disabling \\\"*\\\" through MultiPoint (no default plugins will be automatically registered).\\n221: \\t// Plugins can still be disabled through their individual extension points.\\n222: \\t//\\n223: \\t// In terms of precedence, plugin config follows this basic hierarchy\\n224: \\t//   1. Specific extension points\\n225: \\t//   2. Explicitly configured MultiPoint plugins\\n226: \\t//   3. The set of default plugins, as MultiPoint plugins\\n227: \\t// This implies that a higher precedence plugin will run first and overwrite any settings within MultiPoint.\\n228: \\t// Explicitly user-configured plugins also take a higher precedence over default plugins.\\n229: \\t// Within this hierarchy, an Enabled setting takes precedence over Disabled. For example, if a plugin is\\n230: \\t// set in both `multiPoint.Enabled` and `multiPoint.Disabled`, the plugin will be enabled. Similarly,\\n231: \\t// including `multiPoint.Disabled = '*'` and `multiPoint.Enabled = pluginA` will still register that specific\\n232: \\t// plugin through MultiPoint. This follows the same behavior as all other extension point configurations.\\n233: \\tMultiPoint PluginSet `json:\\\"multiPoint,omitempty\\\"`\\n234: }\\n235: \\n236: // PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\\n237: // If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\\n238: type PluginSet struct {\\n239: \\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\\n240: \\t// If the default plugin is also configured in the scheduler config file, the weight of plugin will\\n241: \\t// be overridden accordingly.\\n242: \\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\\n243: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n244: \\tEnabled []Plugin `json:\\\"enabled,omitempty\\\"`\\n245: \\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\\n246: \\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \\\"*\\\" should be provided.\\n247: \\t// +listType=map\\n248: \\t// +listMapKey=name\\n249: \\tDisabled []Plugin `json:\\\"disabled,omitempty\\\"`\\n250: }\\n251: \\n252: // Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\\n253: type Plugin struct {\\n254: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin\\n255: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n256: \\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\\n257: \\tWeight *int32 `json:\\\"weight,omitempty\\\"`\\n258: }\\n259: \\n260: // PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\\n261: // A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n262: // It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\\n263: type PluginConfig struct {\\n264: \\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\\n265: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n266: \\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\\n267: \\tArgs runtime.RawExtension `json:\\\"args,omitempty\\\"`\\n268: }\\n269: \\n270: func (c *PluginConfig) decodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\\n271: \\tgvk := SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(c.Name + \\\"Args\\\")\\n272: \\t// dry-run to detect and skip out-of-tree plugin args.\\n273: \\tif _, _, err := d.Decode(nil, &gvk, nil); runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err) {\\n274: \\t\\treturn nil\\n275: \\t}\\n276: \\n277: \\tvar strictDecodingErr error\\n278: \\tobj, parsedGvk, err := d.Decode(c.Args.Raw, &gvk, nil)\\n279: \\tif err != nil {\\n280: \\t\\tdecodingArgsErr := fmt.Errorf(\\\"decoding args for plugin %s: %w\\\", c.Name, err)\\n281: \\t\\tif obj != nil && runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\\n282: \\t\\t\\tstrictDecodingErr = runtime.NewStrictDecodingError([]error{decodingArgsErr})\\n283: \\t\\t} else {\\n284: \\t\\t\\treturn decodingArgsErr\\n285: \\t\\t}\\n286: \\t}\\n287: \\tif parsedGvk.GroupKind() != gvk.GroupKind() {\\n288: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"args for plugin %s were not of type %s, got %s\\\", c.Name, gvk.GroupKind(), parsedGvk.GroupKind())\\n289: \\t}\\n290: \\tc.Args.Object = obj\\n291: \\treturn strictDecodingErr\\n292: }\\n293: \\n294: func (c *PluginConfig) encodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\\n295: \\tif c.Args.Object == nil {\\n296: \\t\\treturn nil\\n297: \\t}\\n298: \\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\\n299: \\terr := e.Encode(c.Args.Object, &buf)\\n300: \\tif err != nil {\\n301: \\t\\treturn err\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\t// The <e> encoder might be a YAML encoder, but the parent encoder expects\\n304: \\t// JSON output, so we convert YAML back to JSON.\\n305: \\t// This is a no-op if <e> produces JSON.\\n306: \\tjson, err := yaml.YAMLToJSON(buf.Bytes())\\n307: \\tif err != nil {\\n308: \\t\\treturn err\\n309: \\t}\\n310: \\tc.Args.Raw = json\\n311: \\treturn nil\\n312: }\\n313: \\n314: // Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\\n315: // it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\\n316: type Extender struct {\\n317: \\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\\n318: \\tURLPrefix string `json:\\\"urlPrefix\\\"`\\n319: \\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\\n320: \\tFilterVerb string `json:\\\"filterVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n321: \\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\\n322: \\tPreemptVerb string `json:\\\"preemptVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n323: \\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\\n324: \\tPrioritizeVerb string `json:\\\"prioritizeVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n325: \\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\\n326: \\t// The weight should be a positive integer\\n327: \\tWeight int64 `json:\\\"weight,omitempty\\\"`\\n328: \\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\\n329: \\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\\n330: \\t// can implement this function.\\n331: \\tBindVerb string `json:\\\"bindVerb,omitempty\\\"`\\n332: \\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\\n333: \\tEnableHTTPS bool `json:\\\"enableHTTPS,omitempty\\\"`\\n334: \\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\\n335: \\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig `json:\\\"tlsConfig,omitempty\\\"`\\n336: \\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\\n337: \\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\\n338: \\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration `json:\\\"httpTimeout,omitempty\\\"`\\n339: \\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\\n340: \\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\\n341: \\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\\n342: \\tNodeCacheCapable bool `json:\\\"nodeCacheCapable,omitempty\\\"`\\n343: \\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\\n344: \\t// this extender.\\n345: \\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\\n346: \\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\\n347: \\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\\n348: \\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\\n349: \\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\\n350: \\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\\n351: \\t// +optional\\n352: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n353: \\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource `json:\\\"managedResources,omitempty\\\"`\\n354: \\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\\n355: \\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\\n356: \\tIgnorable bool `json:\\\"ignorable,omitempty\\\"`\\n357: }\\n358: \\n359: // ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\\n360: // managed by an extender.\\n361: type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\\n362: \\t// Name is the extended resource name.\\n363: \\tName string `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n364: \\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\\n365: \\t// resource when applying predicates.\\n366: \\tIgnoredByScheduler bool `json:\\\"ignoredByScheduler,omitempty\\\"`\\n367: }\\n368: \\n369: // ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\\n370: type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\\n371: \\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\\n372: \\tInsecure bool `json:\\\"insecure,omitempty\\\"`\\n373: \\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\\n374: \\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\\n375: \\t// server is used.\\n376: \\tServerName string `json:\\\"serverName,omitempty\\\"`\\n377: \\n378: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n379: \\tCertFile string `json:\\\"certFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n380: \\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\\n381: \\tKeyFile string `json:\\\"keyFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n382: \\t// Trusted root certificates for server\\n383: \\tCAFile string `json:\\\"caFile,omitempty\\\"`\\n384: \\n385: \\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\\n386: \\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\\n387: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n388: \\tCertData []byte `json:\\\"certData,omitempty\\\"`\\n389: \\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\\n390: \\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\\n391: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n392: \\tKeyData []byte `json:\\\"keyData,omitempty\\\"`\\n393: \\t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\\n394: \\t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\\n395: \\t// +listType=atomic\\n396: \\tCAData []byte `json:\\\"caData,omitempty\\\"`\\n397: }\\n398:
