If this is true and leader election is enabled,\n    94→\t// the scheduler will wait to fill informer caches until it is the leader. Doing so will have slower\n    95→\t// failover with the benefit of lower memory overhead while waiting to become leader.\n    96→\t// Defaults to false.\n    97→\tDelayCacheUntilActive bool `json:\"delayCacheUntilActive,omitempty\"`\n    98→}\n    99→\n   100→// DecodeNestedObjects decodes plugin args for known types.\n   101→func (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) DecodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\n   102→\tvar strictDecodingErrs []error\n   103→\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n   104→\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n   105→\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n   106→\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].decodeNestedObjects(d)\n   107→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   108→\t\t\t\tdecodingErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding .profiles[%d].pluginConfig[%d]: %w\", i, j, err)\n   109→\t\t\t\tif runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n   110→\t\t\t\t\tstrictDecodingErrs = append(strictDecodingErrs, decodingErr)\n   111→\t\t\t\t} else {\n   112→\t\t\t\t\treturn decodingErr\n   113→\t\t\t\t}\n   114→\t\t\t}\n   115→\t\t}\n   116→\t}\n   117→\tif len(strictDecodingErrs) > 0 {\n   118→\t\treturn runtime.NewStrictDecodingError(strictDecodingErrs)\n   119→\t}\n   120→\treturn nil\n   121→}\n   122→\n   123→// EncodeNestedObjects encodes plugin args.\n   124→func (c *KubeSchedulerConfiguration) EncodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n   125→\tfor i := range c.Profiles {\n   126→\t\tprof := &c.Profiles[i]\n   127→\t\tfor j := range prof.PluginConfig {\n   128→\t\t\terr := prof.PluginConfig[j].encodeNestedObjects(e)\n   129→\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   130→\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"encoding .profiles[%d].pluginConfig[%d]: %w\", i, j, err)\n   131→\t\t\t}\n   132→\t\t}\n   133→\t}\n   134→\treturn nil\n   135→}\n   136→\n   137→// KubeSchedulingProfile is a scheduling profile.\n   138→type KubeSchedulingProfile struct {\n   139→\t// SchedulerName is the name of the scheduler associated to this profile.\n   140→\t// If SchedulerName matches with the pod's \"spec.schedulerName\", then the pod\n   141→\t// is scheduled with this profile.\n   142→\tSchedulerName *string `json:\"schedulerName,omitempty\"`\n   143→\n   144→\t// PercentageOfNodesToScore is the percentage of all nodes that once found feasible\n   145→\t// for running a pod, the scheduler stops its search for more feasible nodes in\n   146→\t// the cluster. This helps improve scheduler's performance. Scheduler always tries to find\n   147→\t// at least \"minFeasibleNodesToFind\" feasible nodes no matter what the value of this flag is.\n   148→\t// Example: if the cluster size is 500 nodes and the value of this flag is 30,\n   149→\t// then scheduler stops finding further feasible nodes once it finds 150 feasible ones.\n   150→\t// When the value is 0, default percentage (5%--50% based on the size of the cluster) of the\n   151→\t// nodes will be scored. It will override global PercentageOfNodesToScore. If it is empty,\n   152→\t// global PercentageOfNodesToScore will be used.\n   153→\tPercentageOfNodesToScore *int32 `json:\"percentageOfNodesToScore,omitempty\"`\n   154→\n   155→\t// Plugins specify the set of plugins that should be enabled or disabled.\n   156→\t// Enabled plugins are the ones that should be enabled in addition to the\n   157→\t// default plugins. Disabled plugins are any of the default plugins that\n   158→\t// should be disabled.\n   159→\t// When no enabled or disabled plugin is specified for an extension point,\n   160→\t// default plugins for that extension point will be used if there is any.\n   161→\t// If a QueueSort plugin is specified, the same QueueSort Plugin and\n   162→\t// PluginConfig must be specified for all profiles.\n   163→\tPlugins *Plugins `json:\"plugins,omitempty\"`\n   164→\n   165→\t// PluginConfig is an optional set of custom plugin arguments for each plugin.\n   166→\t// Omitting config args for a plugin is equivalent to using the default config\n   167→\t// for that plugin.\n   168→\t// +listType=map\n   169→\t// +listMapKey=name\n   170→\tPluginConfig []PluginConfig `json:\"pluginConfig,omitempty\"`\n   171→}\n   172→\n   173→// Plugins include multiple extension points. When specified, the list of plugins for\n   174→// a particular extension point are the only ones enabled. If an extension point is\n   175→// omitted from the config, then the default set of plugins is used for that extension point.\n   176→// Enabled plugins are called in the order specified here, after default plugins. If they need to\n   177→// be invoked before default plugins, default plugins must be disabled and re-enabled here in desired order.\n   178→type Plugins struct {\n   179→\t// PreEnqueue is a list of plugins that should be invoked before adding pods to the scheduling queue.\n   180→\tPreEnqueue PluginSet `json:\"preEnqueue,omitempty\"`\n   181→\n   182→\t// QueueSort is a list of plugins that should be invoked when sorting pods in the scheduling queue.\n   183→\tQueueSort PluginSet `json:\"queueSort,omitempty\"`\n   184→\n   185→\t// PreFilter is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"PreFilter\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   186→\tPreFilter PluginSet `json:\"preFilter,omitempty\"`\n   187→\n   188→\t// Filter is a list of plugins that should be invoked when filtering out nodes that cannot run the Pod.\n   189→\tFilter PluginSet `json:\"filter,omitempty\"`\n   190→\n   191→\t// PostFilter is a list of plugins that are invoked after filtering phase, but only when no feasible nodes were found for the pod.\n   192→\tPostFilter PluginSet `json:\"postFilter,omitempty\"`\n   193→\n   194→\t// PreScore is a list of plugins that are invoked before scoring.\n   195→\tPreScore PluginSet `json:\"preScore,omitempty\"`\n   196→\n   197→\t// Score is a list of plugins that should be invoked when ranking nodes that have passed the filtering phase.\n   198→\tScore PluginSet `json:\"score,omitempty\"`\n   199→\n   200→\t// Reserve is a list of plugins invoked when reserving/unreserving resources\n   201→\t// after a node is assigned to run the pod.\n   202→\tReserve PluginSet `json:\"reserve,omitempty\"`\n   203→\n   204→\t// Permit is a list of plugins that control binding of a Pod. These plugins can prevent or delay binding of a Pod.\n   205→\tPermit PluginSet `json:\"permit,omitempty\"`\n   206→\n   207→\t// PreBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked before a pod is bound.\n   208→\tPreBind PluginSet `json:\"preBind,omitempty\"`\n   209→\n   210→\t// Bind is a list of plugins that should be invoked at \"Bind\" extension point of the scheduling framework.\n   211→\t// The scheduler call these plugins in order. Scheduler skips the rest of these plugins as soon as one returns success.\n   212→\tBind PluginSet `json:\"bind,omitempty\"`\n   213→\n   214→\t// PostBind is a list of plugins that should be invoked after a pod is successfully bound.\n   215→\tPostBind PluginSet `json:\"postBind,omitempty\"`\n   216→\n   217→\t// MultiPoint is a simplified config section to enable plugins for all valid extension points.\n   218→\t// Plugins enabled through MultiPoint will automatically register for every individual extension\n   219→\t// point the plugin has implemented. Disabling a plugin through MultiPoint disables that behavior.\n   220→\t// The same is true for disabling \"*\" through MultiPoint (no default plugins will be automatically registered).\n   221→\t// Plugins can still be disabled through their individual extension points.\n   222→\t//\n   223→\t// In terms of precedence, plugin config follows this basic hierarchy\n   224→\t//   1. Specific extension points\n   225→\t//   2. Explicitly configured MultiPoint plugins\n   226→\t//   3. The set of default plugins, as MultiPoint plugins\n   227→\t// This implies that a higher precedence plugin will run first and overwrite any settings within MultiPoint.\n   228→\t// Explicitly user-configured plugins also take a higher precedence over default plugins.\n   229→\t// Within this hierarchy, an Enabled setting takes precedence over Disabled. For example, if a plugin is\n   230→\t// set in both `multiPoint.Enabled` and `multiPoint.Disabled`, the plugin will be enabled. Similarly,\n   231→\t// including `multiPoint.Disabled = '*'` and `multiPoint.Enabled = pluginA` will still register that specific\n   232→\t// plugin through MultiPoint. This follows the same behavior as all other extension point configurations.\n   233→\tMultiPoint PluginSet `json:\"multiPoint,omitempty\"`\n   234→}\n   235→\n   236→// PluginSet specifies enabled and disabled plugins for an extension point.\n   237→// If an array is empty, missing, or nil, default plugins at that extension point will be used.\n   238→type PluginSet struct {\n   239→\t// Enabled specifies plugins that should be enabled in addition to default plugins.\n   240→\t// If the default plugin is also configured in the scheduler config file, the weight of plugin will\n   241→\t// be overridden accordingly.\n   242→\t// These are called after default plugins and in the same order specified here.\n   243→\t// +listType=atomic\n   244→\tEnabled []Plugin `json:\"enabled,omitempty\"`\n   245→\t// Disabled specifies default plugins that should be disabled.\n   246→\t// When all default plugins need to be disabled, an array containing only one \"*\" should be provided.\n   247→\t// +listType=map\n   248→\t// +listMapKey=name\n   249→\tDisabled []Plugin `json:\"disabled,omitempty\"`\n   250→}\n   251→\n   252→// Plugin specifies a plugin name and its weight when applicable. Weight is used only for Score plugins.\n   253→type Plugin struct {\n   254→\t// Name defines the name of plugin\n   255→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   256→\t// Weight defines the weight of plugin, only used for Score plugins.\n   257→\tWeight *int32 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n   258→}\n   259→\n   260→// PluginConfig specifies arguments that should be passed to a plugin at the time of initialization.\n   261→// A plugin that is invoked at multiple extension points is initialized once. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   262→// It is up to the plugin to process these Args.\n   263→type PluginConfig struct {\n   264→\t// Name defines the name of plugin being configured\n   265→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   266→\t// Args defines the arguments passed to the plugins at the time of initialization. Args can have arbitrary structure.\n   267→\tArgs runtime.RawExtension `json:\"args,omitempty\"`\n   268→}\n   269→\n   270→func (c *PluginConfig) decodeNestedObjects(d runtime.Decoder) error {\n   271→\tgvk := SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind(c.Name + \"Args\")\n   272→\t// dry-run to detect and skip out-of-tree plugin args.\n   273→\tif _, _, err := d.Decode(nil, &gvk, nil); runtime.IsNotRegisteredError(err) {\n   274→\t\treturn nil\n   275→\t}\n   276→\n   277→\tvar strictDecodingErr error\n   278→\tobj, parsedGvk, err := d.Decode(c.Args.Raw, &gvk, nil)\n   279→\tif err != nil {\n   280→\t\tdecodingArgsErr := fmt.Errorf(\"decoding args for plugin %s: %w\", c.Name, err)\n   281→\t\tif obj != nil && runtime.IsStrictDecodingError(err) {\n   282→\t\t\tstrictDecodingErr = runtime.NewStrictDecodingError([]error{decodingArgsErr})\n   283→\t\t} else {\n   284→\t\t\treturn decodingArgsErr\n   285→\t\t}\n   286→\t}\n   287→\tif parsedGvk.GroupKind() != gvk.GroupKind() {\n   288→\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"args for plugin %s were not of type %s, got %s\", c.Name, gvk.GroupKind(), parsedGvk.GroupKind())\n   289→\t}\n   290→\tc.Args.Object = obj\n   291→\treturn strictDecodingErr\n   292→}\n   293→\n   294→func (c *PluginConfig) encodeNestedObjects(e runtime.Encoder) error {\n   295→\tif c.Args.Object == nil {\n   296→\t\treturn nil\n   297→\t}\n   298→\tvar buf bytes.Buffer\n   299→\terr := e.Encode(c.Args.Object, &buf)\n   300→\tif err != nil {\n   301→\t\treturn err\n   302→\t}\n   303→\t// The <e> encoder might be a YAML encoder, but the parent encoder expects\n   304→\t// JSON output, so we convert YAML back to JSON.\n   305→\t// This is a no-op if <e> produces JSON.\n   306→\tjson, err := yaml.YAMLToJSON(buf.Bytes())\n   307→\tif err != nil {\n   308→\t\treturn err\n   309→\t}\n   310→\tc.Args.Raw = json\n   311→\treturn nil\n   312→}\n   313→\n   314→// Extender holds the parameters used to communicate with the extender. If a verb is unspecified/empty,\n   315→// it is assumed that the extender chose not to provide that extension.\n   316→type Extender struct {\n   317→\t// URLPrefix at which the extender is available\n   318→\tURLPrefix string `json:\"urlPrefix\"`\n   319→\t// Verb for the filter call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the filter call to extender.\n   320→\tFilterVerb string `json:\"filterVerb,omitempty\"`\n   321→\t// Verb for the preempt call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the preempt call to extender.\n   322→\tPreemptVerb string `json:\"preemptVerb,omitempty\"`\n   323→\t// Verb for the prioritize call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the prioritize call to extender.\n   324→\tPrioritizeVerb string `json:\"prioritizeVerb,omitempty\"`\n   325→\t// The numeric multiplier for the node scores that the prioritize call generates.\n   326→\t// The weight should be a positive integer\n   327→\tWeight int64 `json:\"weight,omitempty\"`\n   328→\t// Verb for the bind call, empty if not supported. This verb is appended to the URLPrefix when issuing the bind call to extender.\n   329→\t// If this method is implemented by the extender, it is the extender's responsibility to bind the pod to apiserver. Only one extender\n   330→\t// can implement this function.\n   331→\tBindVerb string `json:\"bindVerb,omitempty\"`\n   332→\t// EnableHTTPS specifies whether https should be used to communicate with the extender\n   333→\tEnableHTTPS bool `json:\"enableHTTPS,omitempty\"`\n   334→\t// TLSConfig specifies the transport layer security config\n   335→\tTLSConfig *ExtenderTLSConfig `json:\"tlsConfig,omitempty\"`\n   336→\t// HTTPTimeout specifies the timeout duration for a call to the extender. Filter timeout fails the scheduling of the pod. Prioritize\n   337→\t// timeout is ignored, k8s/other extenders priorities are used to select the node.\n   338→\tHTTPTimeout metav1.Duration `json:\"httpTimeout,omitempty\"`\n   339→\t// NodeCacheCapable specifies that the extender is capable of caching node information,\n   340→\t// so the scheduler should only send minimal information about the eligible nodes\n   341→\t// assuming that the extender already cached full details of all nodes in the cluster\n   342→\tNodeCacheCapable bool `json:\"nodeCacheCapable,omitempty\"`\n   343→\t// ManagedResources is a list of extended resources that are managed by\n   344→\t// this extender.\n   345→\t// - A pod will be sent to the extender on the Filter, Prioritize and Bind\n   346→\t//   (if the extender is the binder) phases iff the pod requests at least\n   347→\t//   one of the extended resources in this list. If empty or unspecified,\n   348→\t//   all pods will be sent to this extender.\n   349→\t// - If IgnoredByScheduler is set to true for a resource, kube-scheduler\n   350→\t//   will skip checking the resource in predicates.\n   351→\t// +optional\n   352→\t// +listType=atomic\n   353→\tManagedResources []ExtenderManagedResource `json:\"managedResources,omitempty\"`\n   354→\t// Ignorable specifies if the extender is ignorable, i.e. scheduling should not\n   355→\t// fail when the extender returns an error or is not reachable.\n   356→\tIgnorable bool `json:\"ignorable,omitempty\"`\n   357→}\n   358→\n   359→// ExtenderManagedResource describes the arguments of extended resources\n   360→// managed by an extender.\n   361→type ExtenderManagedResource struct {\n   362→\t// Name is the extended resource name.\n   363→\tName string `json:\"name\"`\n   364→\t// IgnoredByScheduler indicates whether kube-scheduler should ignore this\n   365→\t// resource when applying predicates.\n   366→\tIgnoredByScheduler bool `json:\"ignoredByScheduler,omitempty\"`\n   367→}\n   368→\n   369→// ExtenderTLSConfig contains settings to enable TLS with extender\n   370→type ExtenderTLSConfig struct {\n   371→\t// Server should be accessed without verifying the TLS certificate. For testing only.\n   372→\tInsecure bool `json:\"insecure,omitempty\"`\n   373→\t// ServerName is passed to the server for SNI and is used in the client to check server\n   374→\t// certificates against. If ServerName is empty, the hostname used to contact the\n   375→\t// server is used.\n   376→\tServerName string `json:\"serverName,omitempty\"`\n   377→\n   378→\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n   379→\tCertFile string `json:\"certFile,omitempty\"`\n   380→\t// Server requires TLS client certificate authentication\n   381→\tKeyFile string `json:\"keyFile,omitempty\"`\n   382→\t// Trusted root certificates for server\n   383→\tCAFile string `json:\"caFile,omitempty\"`\n   384→\n   385→\t// CertData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate file).\n   386→\t// CertData takes precedence over CertFile\n   387→\t// +listType=atomic\n   388→\tCertData []byte `json:\"certData,omitempty\"`\n   389→\t// KeyData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a client certificate key file).\n   390→\t// KeyData takes precedence over KeyFile\n   391→\t// +listType=atomic\n   392→\tKeyData []byte `json:\"keyData,omitempty\"`\n   393→\t// CAData holds PEM-encoded bytes (typically read from a root certificates bundle).\n   394→\t// CAData takes precedence over CAFile\n   395→\t// +listType=atomic\n   396→\tCAData []byte `json:\"caData,omitempty\"`\n   397→}\n   398→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
